name: Speedrun Biology e2e test
note: A curated end-to-end bundle: 15 hand-authored MCAT biology concept cards (tagged 'biology') paired with 6 topic-matched held-out MMLU biology questions. Cards state facts; questions test application of the same ideas in new words, so the memory->performance bridge is measured, not leaked. Questions are open-licensed (MMLU, MIT).

cards.front: Which second messenger releases Ca2+ from the endoplasmic reticulum into the cytoplasm?
cards.back: IP3 (inositol triphosphate). It binds ER receptors to release Ca2+; DAG activates protein kinase C. cAMP is a separate second messenger.

cards.front: In the carbon cycle, which single process REMOVES CO2 from the atmosphere?
cards.back: Photosynthesis fixes atmospheric CO2 into organic molecules. Respiration, decomposition, and combustion all release CO2.

cards.front: Which macromolecules are found inside the eukaryotic nucleus?
cards.back: DNA, RNA, and protein (histones and other nuclear proteins) are all present.

cards.front: Which biome has the fastest nutrient cycling and highest soil leaching?
cards.back: Tropical rain forest. Warm, wet conditions drive rapid decomposition and turnover, leaving nutrient-poor soils.

cards.front: Which cellular structure most likely arose first in the earliest cells, and why?
cards.back: The plasma membrane. A lipid bilayer boundary is required to separate internal chemistry from the environment.

cards.front: Which xylem cells undergo programmed cell death to become functional?
cards.back: Vessel members and tracheids lose their cytoplasm, leaving hollow tubes for water transport.

cards.front: What key feature distinguishes angiosperms from gymnosperms?
cards.back: Angiosperms enclose seeds within an ovary (fruit) and produce flowers; gymnosperms bear naked seeds (e.g., cones).

cards.front: What does an enzyme do to a reaction's activation energy?
cards.back: It lowers the activation energy, speeding the reaction without being consumed or shifting the equilibrium.

cards.front: Contrast passive and active membrane transport.
cards.back: Passive transport moves solutes down their gradient with no ATP (diffusion, osmosis, facilitated diffusion); active transport uses ATP to move against the gradient.

cards.front: Is DNA replication conservative, semiconservative, or dispersive?
cards.back: Semiconservative: each daughter duplex keeps one parental (template) strand and one newly synthesized strand.

cards.front: How do the products of mitosis and meiosis differ?
cards.back: Mitosis yields two diploid, genetically identical cells; meiosis yields four haploid, genetically varied gametes.

cards.front: Where does the electron transport chain occur, and what does it produce?
cards.back: The inner mitochondrial membrane. It builds a proton gradient that ATP synthase uses to make most of the cell's ATP (oxidative phosphorylation).

cards.front: Distinguish the light-dependent reactions from the Calvin cycle in photosynthesis.
cards.back: Light reactions (thylakoid membranes) make ATP and NADPH and release O2; the Calvin cycle (stroma) uses them to fix CO2 into sugar.

cards.front: State Mendel's law of independent assortment.
cards.back: Alleles of different genes assort into gametes independently, for genes on different (unlinked) chromosomes.

cards.front: What is negative feedback in homeostasis? Give an example.
cards.back: A response that counteracts a change to restore the set point, e.g., insulin lowering elevated blood glucose.

questions.stem: Which of the following is a second messenger that stimulates release of calcium ions into the cytoplasm?
questions.explanation: IP3 binds receptors on the endoplasmic reticulum, releasing stored Ca2+ into the cytoplasm.

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT a source of atmospheric carbon?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis removes CO2 from the atmosphere; the others release it.

questions.stem: The soils of which of the following biomes has the highest rate of leaching and cycling of nutrients?
questions.explanation: Warm, wet tropical rain forests decompose and cycle nutrients fastest, leaching soils.

questions.stem: In the formation of the earliest cells, which of the following components most likely arose first?
questions.explanation: A boundary membrane is prerequisite to concentrating and protecting internal chemistry.

questions.stem: Which of the following plant cells undergoes programmed cell death to become functional?
questions.explanation: Xylem vessel members die and empty, forming hollow conduits for water transport.

questions.stem: Which of the following characteristics distinguishes angiosperms from gymnosperms?
questions.explanation: Angiosperms enclose seeds within ovaries (fruit); gymnosperm seeds are naked.
